creator: driesm@freebsd.org
text:
Ok I was confused, this was my bad. The title is the only step I had to do manually to run "calibre" in "server daemon" mode. The RC script suffices which is already installed as part of calibre.

The idea after the "server" mode is that calibre is run as the "calibre user" or any other user designated to run the daemon really like a service, not as a user already present on the system. Calibre is never run by a user in this way in an X or Wayland env. The only interaction with calibre is through the CLI and the web interface.

Basically I just had to modify already present variables from the rc script to run eg the calibre database from /var/db/calibre, instead of from a users home directory.

For me this bug can be closed, as all functionality is already present (except that a daemon user is installed with the port, but this can be done manually too).
